name: compiler
context: Compile, type-check, and build TypeScript projects with tsc â€” watch mode, build mode, diagnostics, and emit control
info: The TypeScript compiler (tsc) is the official CLI tool for type-checking and compiling TypeScript to JavaScript. It reads tsconfig.json for configuration, supports watch mode for development, build mode for multi-project workspaces, and incremental compilation for fast rebuilds. tsc performs three phases: parsing source files into an AST, running the type checker to validate types and infer missing annotations, and emitting JavaScript output plus optional declaration files (.d.ts) and source maps. Agents use tsc to validate code correctness (--noEmit), generate distributable JavaScript (--outDir), manage multi-project builds (--build), and diagnose type errors. See [[typescript/config]] for tsconfig.json options and [[typescript/project-refs]] for composite project builds.
body:
# TypeScript Compiler (tsc)

The `tsc` CLI is the official TypeScript compiler. It type-checks `.ts` and `.tsx` files, reports errors, and emits JavaScript output.

## Installation

```bash
# Install as a dev dependency (recommended)
npm install -D typescript

# Run via npx
npx tsc --version

# Or install globally
npm install -g typescript
tsc --version
```

## Core Commands

```bash
# Initialize tsconfig.json with defaults
npx tsc --init

# Type-check only (no JavaScript output)
npx tsc --noEmit

# Compile using tsconfig.json
npx tsc

# Compile specific files (ignores tsconfig)
npx tsc src/index.ts src/utils.ts

# Compile with inline options
npx tsc --target es2022 --module esnext --outDir dist src/index.ts

# Compile with a specific tsconfig
npx tsc --project tsconfig.build.json
npx tsc -p tsconfig.build.json
```

## Watch Mode

Watch mode recompiles on file changes. Use during development.

```bash
# Watch mode with default tsconfig
npx tsc --watch
npx tsc -w

# Watch with type-check only
npx tsc --watch --noEmit

# Watch with a specific config
npx tsc -w -p tsconfig.dev.json
```

Watch mode uses file system events by default. On systems without reliable fs events, set `TSC_WATCHFILE=DynamicPriorityPolling` for polling.

## Build Mode (--build)

Build mode compiles project references â€” multiple tsconfig files linked as a dependency graph. Essential for monorepos.

```bash
# Build the current project and all its references
npx tsc --build
npx tsc -b

# Build a specific tsconfig
npx tsc -b tsconfig.build.json

# Build multiple projects
npx tsc -b packages/core packages/cli packages/web

# Build in verbose mode
npx tsc -b --verbose

# Force rebuild (ignore incremental cache)
npx tsc -b --force

# Clean build outputs
npx tsc -b --clean

# Dry run (show what would be built)
npx tsc -b --dry

# Watch mode with build
npx tsc -b --watch
```

See [[typescript/project-refs]] for configuring composite projects.

## Emit Control

Control what tsc outputs:

```bash
# Emit to a specific directory
npx tsc --outDir dist

# Generate declaration files (.d.ts)
npx tsc --declaration

# Generate declaration maps (for go-to-definition into .ts source)
npx tsc --declaration --declarationMap

# Generate source maps
npx tsc --sourceMap

# Inline source maps in the JS output
npx tsc --inlineSourceMap

# Skip emit on errors
npx tsc --noEmitOnError

# Type-check only, no output files
npx tsc --noEmit

# Skip type checking, only emit (5.6+)
npx tsc --noCheck

# Remove comments from output
npx tsc --removeComments

# Emit only declaration files
npx tsc --emitDeclarationOnly
```

## Incremental Compilation

Incremental mode caches type-checking results for faster rebuilds:

```bash
# Enable incremental compilation
npx tsc --incremental

# Specify the cache file location
npx tsc --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .cache/tsconfig.tsbuildinfo
```

This writes a `.tsbuildinfo` file. On subsequent runs, tsc skips re-checking files that haven't changed.

## Diagnostics and Debugging

```bash
# Show all compiler options
npx tsc --all

# Show effective configuration
npx tsc --showConfig

# Explain why a file is included
npx tsc --explainFiles

# Generate a trace for performance analysis
npx tsc --generateTrace ./trace

# Extended diagnostics
npx tsc --extendedDiagnostics

# List files that would be compiled
npx tsc --listFiles

# List emitted files
npx tsc --listEmittedFiles

# Pretty-print errors (default on TTY)
npx tsc --pretty
```

## Common Error Patterns and Fixes

| Error Code | Message | Fix |
|------------|---------|-----|
| TS2307 | Cannot find module 'X' | Install `@types/X` or add module declaration |
| TS2339 | Property 'X' does not exist on type 'Y' | Check spelling, add to interface, or narrow the type |
| TS2345 | Argument of type 'X' is not assignable to parameter of type 'Y' | Fix the type mismatch or use a type assertion |
| TS2322 | Type 'X' is not assignable to type 'Y' | Ensure shapes match or widen the target type |
| TS7006 | Parameter 'X' implicitly has an 'any' type | Add a type annotation or set `noImplicitAny: false` |
| TS1259 | Module 'X' can only be default-imported using esModuleInterop | Set `esModuleInterop: true` in tsconfig |
| TS6133 | 'X' is declared but its value is never read | Remove unused variable or prefix with `_` |
| TS18046 | 'X' is of type 'unknown' | Narrow with `typeof`, `instanceof`, or type guard |

## Integration with Other Tools

tsc is often used for type-checking only, while faster tools handle transpilation:

```bash
# esbuild â€” fast bundler, strips types, no type checking
npx esbuild src/index.ts --bundle --outdir=dist --platform=node

# swc â€” Rust-based transpiler, no type checking
npx swc src -d dist

# Vite â€” uses esbuild in dev, rollup in prod
npm create vite@latest my-app -- --template react-ts

# tsx â€” run .ts files directly (uses esbuild)
npx tsx src/index.ts

# Typical CI pipeline: tsc for checking, esbuild for building
npx tsc --noEmit && npx esbuild src/index.ts --bundle --outdir=dist
```

## package.json Scripts

```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit",
    "build": "tsc",
    "build:watch": "tsc --watch",
    "build:clean": "rm -rf dist && tsc",
    "dev": "tsx watch src/index.ts",
    "lint": "tsc --noEmit && eslint src"
  }
}
```

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Purpose |
|----------|----------|
| `TSC_WATCHFILE` | File watching strategy: `UseFsEvents`, `UseFsEventsOnParentDirectory`, `DynamicPriorityPolling` |
| `TSC_NONPOLLING_WATCHER` | Set to `1` to use native file watchers |
| `TS_NODE_PROJECT` | Path to tsconfig.json for ts-node |
| `TS_NODE_COMPILER_OPTIONS` | Override compiler options for ts-node |

sections.title: Type-check only workflow
sections.body: Use `tsc --noEmit` to type-check without producing output files. This is the most common CI usage â€” let tsc validate types while a faster tool (esbuild, swc, Vite) handles the actual build. Add `"typecheck": "tsc --noEmit"` to package.json scripts. Combine with `--incremental` for faster re-checks: `tsc --noEmit --incremental`. In monorepos, use `tsc -b --noEmit` to check all referenced projects. The `--noCheck` flag (5.6+) does the opposite â€” emit without type checking, useful when type checking runs separately.

sections.title: Watch mode
sections.body: Run `tsc --watch` or `tsc -w` for continuous compilation during development. tsc watches all files matched by `include` in tsconfig.json and recompiles on changes. Watch mode uses file system events by default (efficient on macOS/Linux). If fs events are unreliable (networked drives, Docker volumes), set `TSC_WATCHFILE=DynamicPriorityPolling`. Combine with `--preserveWatchOutput` to keep previous output on screen. For build mode projects, use `tsc -b --watch` to watch across project references.

sections.title: Build mode for project references
sections.body: Build mode (`tsc -b` or `tsc --build`) compiles composite projects and their dependencies in the correct order. It reads the `references` array in tsconfig.json and builds the dependency graph. Key flags: `--verbose` (show build steps), `--force` (ignore incremental cache), `--clean` (delete outputs), `--dry` (show what would be built). Build mode requires `composite: true` and `declaration: true` in each referenced project's tsconfig. See [[typescript/project-refs]] for setup patterns.

sections.title: Generating declaration files
sections.body: Declaration files (`.d.ts`) expose type information without implementation code. Generate them with `--declaration`. Add `--declarationMap` so consumers can navigate to your TypeScript source via go-to-definition. Use `--emitDeclarationOnly` when a bundler handles JS emit and you only need types. The `--declarationDir` option specifies where `.d.ts` files are written (defaults to alongside JS output). In `package.json`, point `"types"` or `"typings"` to your main declaration file. For `isolatedDeclarations` (5.5+), each file's exported types must be explicitly annotated â€” no inference from function bodies.

sections.title: Diagnostics and troubleshooting
sections.body: When tsc behavior is confusing, use diagnostic flags. `--showConfig` prints the effective merged configuration (after extends and defaults). `--explainFiles` shows why each file is included in compilation (useful for debugging unexpected includes). `--listFiles` lists all files that will be compiled. `--extendedDiagnostics` shows timing and memory usage for each compilation phase. `--generateTrace ./trace` produces a Chrome-compatible trace file for profiling slow compilations â€” open it in `chrome://tracing` or `edge://tracing`.

scripts.name: Type-check project
scripts.description: Run tsc --noEmit to type-check the project without emitting output. Returns exit code 0 on success, non-zero with error details on failure.

scripts.name: Initialize tsconfig.json
scripts.description: Generate a tsconfig.json with recommended defaults for a modern TypeScript project

build.description: Use build mode for project references

success.description: Whether compilation succeeded with no errors
